project.name: Chessle Recreation
project.purpose: Academic school project to recreate Chessle for educational enrichment
project.description: Wordle applied to chess openings. The player guesses a sequence of moves; color-coded feedback reveals how close each guess was to the target opening. This recreation runs in unlimited mode rather than a daily puzzle format.

current_configuration.note: All configuration values should be stored as single variables to support future customization without major refactoring.

todos.description: Allow the player to set how many full moves (plies) they guess per round. Currently locked to 5 full moves (10 half-moves). Should be driven by a single config variable so the tile grid and opening filter update automatically.
Allow the player to set how many guesses they get per round. Currently locked to 6. The guess row stack should render dynamically based on this value.
After a round ends, generate a shareable emoji grid (green/yellow/gray squares) representing the player's guess history, copyable to clipboard. Identical in format to Wordle share output.

Use the left and right arrow keys to navigate backward and forward through the moves already entered in the current guess row. Previously played moves are temporarily stored so stepping back and forward restores the board position correctly. This allows the player to review and replace moves mid-sequence without fully undoing back to the start.

this_recreation.description: Fixed mode for this project. Move count and guess count will become configurable in future TODOs.

flow: Board loads with all pieces in standard starting position.
Player drags pieces to enter moves, alternating white and black.
Each move must be a legal chess move; illegal moves are rejected.
As each move is made, its algebraic notation fills the next empty tile in the current guess row.
Once all 10 tiles in the current row are filled, the player presses Submit.
Each tile in the submitted row flips to green, yellow, or gray.
The player continues guessing until they match the opening exactly (all green) or exhaust all 6 guesses.
The end-of-game screen appears with the opening name, correct sequence, a Lichess study link, and a Play Again button.
Play Again picks a new random opening and resets the board and all guess rows.

move_input.method: Drag and drop pieces on the chessboard
move_input.alternation: White and black alternate, same as a real game
move_input.validation: Only legal chess moves are accepted
move_input.notation_display: Each move auto-populates the next empty tile in SAN (Standard Algebraic Notation)

green.meaning: Correct move in the correct position
green.condition: Move notation matches exactly AND position index matches

yellow.meaning: Move appears somewhere in the target opening but not at this position
yellow.condition: Move notation appears anywhere in the target sequence, including possibly played by the other side
yellow.note: A yellow move could be played by either white or black in the target opening

gray.meaning: Move does not appear anywhere in the target opening
gray.condition: Move notation is absent from the entire target sequence

feedback_system.evaluation_order: Evaluate green first, then yellow, to avoid double-counting the same move

guess_rows.structure: One row per guess, stacked vertically
guess_rows.history_visibility: All previous guess rows remain visible at all times

controls.submit_button: Submits the current fully-entered guess row
controls.play_again_button: Appears on end-of-game screen; picks a new random opening and resets everything

end_of_game_screen.shown_when: Player solves the puzzle OR exhausts all guesses
displays: Full opening name and variation (e.g. 'King's Indian Defense: Orthodox Variation')
Correct move sequence in algebraic notation
Link to study the opening on the Lichess opening explorer

opening_database.format: TSV files organized by ECO volume (A through E)
opening_database.filtering: Filter to entries with at least 10 half-moves so every puzzle fills all 10 tiles cleanly

selection_strategy.this_recreation: Purely random on each new game (Play Again or page load)
selection_strategy.load_by_id_todo: TODO_4: deterministic selection by pasted game ID/seed string

opening_explorer.description: After each round, a link directs the player to study the target opening on Lichess.
features_available: Play through opening lines move by move
View ECO code and variation name

chessground.description: The chessboard UI library used to render the board and handle piece interaction.
key_features: SVG drawing of circles and arrows

move_legality.purpose: Validate each move before it registers on the board; reject illegal moves

notation_consistency.warning: Comparisons must be exact. 'Nxf3' and 'Nf3' are different moves. Normalize all notation before comparing.
notation_consistency.captures_and_checks: Notation like 'Nxf7' and 'Nxf7+' are not the same and must not be treated as equivalent
notation_consistency.color_discrepancy: Notation like 'e5' played by white IS the same as 'e5' played by black. Do not consider color when comparing moves.

yellow_gray_logic.step_1: For each submitted move, check if it appears anywhere in the target sequence (any index, either color).
yellow_gray_logic.step_2: If yes and the index also matches, mark green.
yellow_gray_logic.step_3: If yes but index does not match, mark yellow.
yellow_gray_logic.step_4: If the move does not appear anywhere in the target sequence, mark gray.
yellow_gray_logic.double_count_prevention: Evaluate and lock greens first before assigning yellows.

play_again.behavior: Randomly selects a new opening from the filtered database, resets the board to starting position, clears all guess rows, and resets guess counter.
play_again.no_repeat_strategy: Optional: track recently played openings in session to avoid immediate repeats.

load_game_by_id.proposed_approach: Assign each opening in the filtered dataset a stable numeric index. The game ID is that index (or a short hash of it). Pasting the ID into a load field seeds the random selector to that specific opening deterministically.

config_variables.note: Both values drive tile grid rendering, opening filter, and guess row count. Change here only to propagate everywhere.
